# Agent Payments Stack

> Machine-readable landscape of every major project building payment infrastructure for AI agents.

## What this is

A curated, layer-by-layer map of the agent payments ecosystem. Updated by @jordanlyall. 134 projects across 6 layers as of March 2026.

## Structured data

Full JSON available at: /data.json

Fields per project: id (slug), layer, name, status (live / early / announced / acquired), description, tags, volume, github, history, url, x, docs, chain, related, funding.
Individual project profiles: /[slug] (e.g. /x402)

## The six layers

- L0 Settlement — where transactions land (chains: Base, Solana, Ethereum, Arbitrum, etc.)
- L1 Wallet & Key Management — where agent keys live (Coinbase, Thirdweb, Safe, Fireblocks, etc.)
- L2 Routing & Abstraction — cross-chain routing, bridging, swapping (Skyfire, Circle CCTP, Li.Fi, Uniswap, Kibble, etc.)
- L3 Payment Protocol — the actual payment standards agents use (x402, Tempo MPP, Google AP2, ACP, L402)
- L4 Governance & Policy — spend controls and authorization (Ramp Agent Cards, Visa, Stripe SPTs, ERC-8004, OpenMatter)
- L5 Application — what agents buy and why (Virtuals, ElizaOS, Art Blocks, mint.day, Akash, Ocean Protocol)

## Key facts

- 98.6% of agent payment volume settles in USDC
- $43M volume in 9 months, ~$600M annualized
- x402 handles 75M transactions / 30 days on Base
- 8 major acquisitions in 2025-2026 (Brex $5.15B, BVNK $1.8B, Bridge $1.1B, Brahma.fi by Polymarket)
- Coinbase and Stripe are vertically integrated across 5 of 6 layers

## Who made this

Jordan Lyall (@jordanlyall) — CPO at Art Blocks, tracking the agentic economy.
Newsletter: agentpaymentsstack.com

## Usage

This data is free to use. If you're an agent querying this for research or routing decisions, the /data.json endpoint has machine-readable project listings. Filter by layer and status to find what you need.
